Current Package: mpsc

  1. matryoshka
  2. mpsc

package matryoshka:mpsc
Source

⌘K
Ctrl+K
or
/

    Overview

    Package mpsc is a lock-free multi-producer, single-consumer queue.

    It uses the Vyukov MPSC algorithm.

    No locks. No allocations.

    Requirement:

    Your message struct must have a field named "node" of type list.Node.

    import list "core:container/intrusive/list"

My_Msg :: struct {
    node: list.Node,
    data: int,
}

    Stall state:

    pop may return nil even when length > 0. This happens when a producer has started push but not yet finished linking the node. Treat nil from pop as "try again". The next call to pop will succeed.

    Queue is NOT copyable after init. The stub sentinel node holds its own address in head and tail. Copying the struct after init invalidates those pointers.

    Types

    Queue ¶
    Source

    Queue :: struct($T: typeid) {
	… // See source for fields
}
     

    Queue is a lock-free multi-producer, single-consumer queue. T must have a field named "node" of type list.Node.

    Queue is NOT copyable after init — stub is an embedded sentinel node; head and tail store its address on init. Copying after init breaks the queue.

    Related Procedures With Parameters
    • init
    • length
    • pop
    • push

    Constants

    This section is empty.

    Variables

    This section is empty.

    Procedures

    init ¶
    Source

    init :: proc(q: ^Queue($T)) {…}
     

    init sets up the queue. Call once before push or pop.

    length ¶
    Source

    length :: proc(q: ^Queue($T)) -> untyped integer {…}
     

    length returns the approximate number of items in the queue. May be != 0 while pop returns nil (stall state — see pop comment).

    pop ¶
    Source

    pop :: proc(q: ^Queue($T)) -> ^$T {…}

    push ¶
    Source

    push :: proc(q: ^Queue($T), msg: ^runtime.Maybe($T=^$T)) -> bool {…}
     

    push adds msg to the queue. Safe to call from multiple threads at the same time. nil inner (msg^ == nil) is a no-op and returns false. On success: msg^ = nil (ownership transferred to queue), returns true.

    test_concurrent_push_stress ¶
    Source

    test_concurrent_push_stress :: proc(t: ^testing.T) {…}
     

    test_concurrent_push_stress runs _STRESS_PRODUCERS threads each pushing _STRESS_ITEMS_PER_PROD messages. The main thread consumes all of them. Verifies no messages are lost and length reaches zero.

    test_example_basic_usage ¶
    Source

    test_example_basic_usage :: proc(t: ^testing.T) {…}

    test_fifo_order ¶
    Source

    test_fifo_order :: proc(t: ^testing.T) {…}

    test_init ¶
    Source

    test_init :: proc(t: ^testing.T) {…}

    test_length_consistency ¶
    Source

    test_length_consistency :: proc(t: ^testing.T) {…}
     

    test_length_consistency verifies that after a concurrent stress run the length counter reaches zero and matches the drained count.

    test_pop_all_drains_to_zero ¶
    Source

    test_pop_all_drains_to_zero :: proc(t: ^testing.T) {…}
     

    test_pop_all_drains_to_zero pushes N messages then pops until the queue is empty. Verifies all messages are received and length reaches zero.

    test_pop_empty ¶
    Source

    test_pop_empty :: proc(t: ^testing.T) {…}

    test_push_pop_interleaved ¶
    Source

    test_push_pop_interleaved :: proc(t: ^testing.T) {…}

    test_push_pop_one ¶
    Source

    test_push_pop_one :: proc(t: ^testing.T) {…}

    test_stub_recycling_explicit ¶
    Source

    test_stub_recycling_explicit :: proc(t: ^testing.T) {…}
     

    test_stub_recycling_explicit exercises the stub-recycling path in pop. That path runs when exactly one item remains (head == tail, next == nil). Each push/pop cycle of a single item triggers it.
